Trigger Condition: Vendor will process personal data of EU/EEA data subjects on behalf of Lichen
Data Types Involved: Any personal data of EU/EEA individuals (name, email, ID, behavioral, device identifiers)
Regulations Triggered: GDPR Art. 28; GDPR Art. 4(8)
Required DPA / Contract Clauses: GDPR Art. 28 DPA; mandatory clauses: subject matter & duration; nature & purpose of processing; data type & data subject categories; obligations & rights of controller
Additional Obligations: Lichen Legal must execute Art. 28 DPA before any processing begins.

Trigger Condition: Vendor will process personal data of California residents and qualifies as a service provider under CCPA
Data Types Involved: Personal information as defined under Cal. Civ. Code §1798.140 (broad — includes inferences, browsing history, identifiers)
Regulations Triggered: CCPA/CPRA §1798.100 et seq.; Cal. Civ. Code §1798.140(ag)
Required DPA / Contract Clauses: CCPA Service Provider Agreement (or addendum); must prohibit: sale or sharing of PI; use for vendor's own commercial purposes; retention beyond contract; combining PI across clients
Additional Obligations: Lichen Legal must attach or execute CCPA Service Provider addendum. Verify vendor has not sold or shared the data in prior 12 months.

Trigger Condition: Vendor will process special-category personal data of EU/EEA data subjects (e.g., health, biometric, union membership)
Data Types Involved: Health/medical data; biometric identifiers; racial or ethnic origin; religious beliefs; criminal conviction data; trade union membership
Regulations Triggered: GDPR Art. 9; GDPR Art. 28; potentially HIPAA if US health data overlaps
Required DPA / Contract Clauses: GDPR Art. 28 DPA with explicit Art. 9 processing provisions; document legal basis under Art. 9(2); DPIA under Art. 35 mandatory; enhanced security obligations
Additional Obligations: DPIA must be completed and documented before engagement. CISO approval required.

Trigger Condition: Vendor will process personal data of minors (under 16 in EU; under 13 or 16 depending on US state law)
Data Types Involved: Personal data where data subject age is known or reasonably inferred to be below applicable threshold
Regulations Triggered: GDPR Art. 8; COPPA (US); UK GDPR Age Appropriate Design Code
Required DPA / Contract Clauses: GDPR Art. 28 DPA; COPPA written agreement if applicable; vendor must implement age-verification or age-appropriate processing controls; parental consent mechanism documentation required
Additional Obligations: Engage Privacy Team and Legal before any processing of minor data. Separate DPIA required.

Trigger Condition: Vendor will process personal data of UK data subjects post-Brexit
Data Types Involved: Any personal data of UK individuals
Regulations Triggered: UK GDPR (retained EU law); Data Protection Act 2018
Required DPA / Contract Clauses: UK GDPR Art. 28 DPA (separate from EU GDPR DPA or combined with appropriate UK-specific addendum); UK ICO standard clauses if applicable
Additional Obligations: Confirm whether EU Art. 28 DPA is sufficient or a UK addendum is required.

Trigger Condition: Vendor will transfer or access EU/EEA personal data from a country without EU adequacy decision (e.g., US, India, non-adequate third country)
Data Types Involved: Any EU/EEA personal data transferred to or accessed from a non-adequate country
Regulations Triggered: GDPR Ch. V (Arts. 44–49); EDPB transfer guidance
Required DPA / Contract Clauses: Standard Contractual Clauses (SCCs) — 2021 EU SCCs (Commission Decision 2021/914); Transfer Impact Assessment (TIA) documenting legal landscape of destination country; Supplementary Technical Measures where TIA identifies residual risk
Additional Obligations: Legal must determine applicable SCC module (C2P or C2C). TIA must be completed and retained.

Trigger Condition: Vendor will transfer UK personal data to a non-adequate third country
Data Types Involved: UK personal data
Regulations Triggered: UK GDPR Art. 46; ICO International Data Transfer Agreement (IDTA)
Required DPA / Contract Clauses: UK IDTA (replaces EU SCCs for UK-origin data post-Brexit) or UK Addendum to EU SCCs; Transfer Risk Assessment (TRA) per ICO guidance
Additional Obligations: Confirm whether EU SCCs + UK Addendum or standalone IDTA is appropriate for the transfer pattern.

Trigger Condition: Vendor is located in an EU-adequate country (e.g., UK pre-adequacy review expiry, Switzerland, Japan, Canada for commercial organizations)
Data Types Involved: Any personal data transferred to an adequate country
Regulations Triggered: GDPR Art. 45; applicable adequacy decision
Required DPA / Contract Clauses: Art. 28 DPA still required if vendor is a processor. SCCs not required if adequacy decision is current and in force. Monitor adequacy status — decisions can be invalidated (cf. Schrems II).
Additional Obligations: Legal to verify adequacy decision is current at time of contract and annually.

Trigger Condition: Vendor will access or process personal data from California under a business purpose but does not meet service provider definition (e.g., limited contract controls)
Data Types Involved: California personal information
Regulations Triggered: CCPA/CPRA — third party or contractor category applies
Required DPA / Contract Clauses: If vendor cannot be classified as a service provider, data sharing may constitute a sale or share under CCPA, triggering opt-out rights and additional disclosure obligations. Contractor agreement required if applicable.
Additional Obligations: Legal to assess whether vendor qualifies as service provider, contractor, or third party. Incorrect classification creates CCPA sale/share liability.

Trigger Condition: Vendor will engage sub-processors to perform any part of the processing activities covered by the DPA
Data Types Involved: Any personal data within scope of the DPA
Regulations Triggered: GDPR Art. 28(2) and (4); contractual flow-down obligation
Required DPA / Contract Clauses: DPA must include: general or specific authorization for sub-processing; obligation on vendor to impose equivalent DPA obligations on sub-processors; notification right for Lichen prior to sub-processor changes; Lichen right to object to new sub-processors
Additional Obligations: Vendor must provide current sub-processor list at contract execution. Lichen IT Security and Legal must review sub-processors handling RESTRICTED data.

Trigger Condition: Vendor proposes to add a new sub-processor after DPA execution
Data Types Involved: Personal data processed under the DPA
Regulations Triggered: GDPR Art. 28(2); DPA contractual terms
Required DPA / Contract Clauses: Vendor must provide advance written notice (minimum 10 business days per Lichen standard terms). Lichen has right to object on reasonable grounds. If objection upheld and vendor cannot remediate, either party may terminate the affected service.
Additional Obligations: IT Security reviews sub-processor security posture upon notification. Legal assesses DPA flow-down adequacy.

Trigger Condition: Vendor will process payment card data (cardholder data or SAD) on behalf of Lichen
Data Types Involved: Primary Account Number (PAN); cardholder name; expiration date; CVV/CVC; PIN blocks
Regulations Triggered: PCI DSS v4.0 (Requirements 12.8–12.9 for third-party service providers)
Required DPA / Contract Clauses: PCI DSS TPSP Agreement or DPA addendum; vendor must provide current PCI DSS Report on Compliance (ROC) or SAQ; annual acknowledgment of PCI responsibilities; must be listed on Lichen's TPSP inventory
Additional Obligations: Confirm vendor's current PCI DSS compliance status before engagement. IT Security to retain copy of ROC/SAQ.

Trigger Condition: Vendor will process protected health information (PHI) or electronic PHI (ePHI) as a business associate
Data Types Involved: PHI as defined under 45 CFR §160.103: individually identifiable health information in any form
Regulations Triggered: HIPAA Privacy Rule (45 CFR Part 164 Subpart E); HIPAA Security Rule (45 CFR Part 164 Subpart C); HITECH Act
Required DPA / Contract Clauses: HIPAA Business Associate Agreement (BAA) — mandatory under 45 CFR §164.308(b); must include: permitted uses and disclosures; safeguard obligations; breach notification obligations; return/destroy obligations at termination
Additional Obligations: HIPAA BAA must be executed before any PHI is disclosed. Legal must confirm vendor is not a conduit (conduits do not require BAA).

Trigger Condition: Vendor will process Lichen employee personal data (HR data, payroll, benefits, disciplinary records)
Data Types Involved: Employee name, national ID/SSN, salary, performance data, disciplinary records, health/benefits data
Regulations Triggered: GDPR Art. 28 (if EU employees); applicable local employment law (e.g., German BDSG §26, French Labor Code); CCPA/CPRA employee PI exemption status
Required DPA / Contract Clauses: Art. 28 DPA; local employment law addenda as required by jurisdiction; employee notification obligations may apply; works council consultation may be required in applicable EU jurisdictions
Additional Obligations: HR and Legal must jointly assess jurisdictional requirements before engagement. Works council review mandatory for applicable EU operations.

Trigger Condition: Vendor will process financial data that is not payment card data (e.g., bank account details, financial forecasts, M&A data)
Data Types Involved: Bank account numbers; ABA routing numbers; financial statements; earnings data
Regulations Triggered: GLBA Safeguards Rule (if financial institution nexus); GDPR Art. 28 if EU personal financial data; SOX data-integrity controls if related to financial reporting
Required DPA / Contract Clauses: DPA or data handling agreement required if personal financial data is in scope; SOX controls addendum if data feeds into financial reporting; confidentiality agreement (NDA) baseline requirement
Additional Obligations: Legal to determine if GLBA or SOX obligations attach. NDA baseline required regardless.

Trigger Condition: Vendor will have logical access to Lichen production systems or data repositories containing personal data
Data Types Involved: Any personal data accessible via the production system or repository
Regulations Triggered: GDPR Art. 28 (if EU personal data); ISP-001 §12 (Lichen IT Security Policy); access control obligations
Required DPA / Contract Clauses: Art. 28 DPA; ISP-001 §12 compliance attestation; role-based access control and least-privilege acknowledgment; access revocation on termination obligation
Additional Obligations: IT Security must provision and review access per ISP-001 §6. DPA executed before access granted.

Trigger Condition: Vendor will retain personal data beyond the term of the services (e.g., for backup, legal hold, or archival purposes)
Data Types Involved: Personal data within scope of the engagement
Regulations Triggered: GDPR Arts. 5(1)(e) (storage limitation); 28(3)(g); applicable retention laws
Required DPA / Contract Clauses: DPA must include: specific retention period or criteria; obligation to delete or return data on termination; certification of deletion required; exceptions for legal hold must be documented and time-limited
Additional Obligations: Legal to define contractual retention period at engagement outset. Post-termination deletion certification must be obtained within 30 days of termination.

Trigger Condition: Vendor will process personal data solely on documented instructions from Lichen (no independent discretion over purpose or means)
Data Types Involved: Any personal data within scope
Regulations Triggered: GDPR Art. 28(3)(a) — processor instruction requirement
Required DPA / Contract Clauses: DPA must include: vendor processes only on documented instructions; vendor notifies Lichen if it believes an instruction infringes GDPR; prohibition on processing for vendor's own purposes
Additional Obligations: Instructions must be documented in the SOW or DPA Annex. Legal to confirm instruction language is sufficiently specific.

Trigger Condition: Vendor provides a SaaS platform where Lichen uploads or generates personal data (Lichen is controller; vendor is processor of that data)
Data Types Involved: Any personal data uploaded to or generated within the SaaS platform
Regulations Triggered: GDPR Art. 28; CCPA service provider provisions; vendor's own privacy policy must not conflict
Required DPA / Contract Clauses: Art. 28 DPA or vendor's standard DPA (reviewed and approved by Lichen Legal); ensure vendor DPA does not grant vendor rights to use Lichen data for model training, analytics, or product improvement without explicit consent
Additional Obligations: Legal must review vendor's standard DPA — do not accept without review. Flag any model-training or product-improvement carve-outs.

Trigger Condition: Vendor will conduct profiling or automated decision-making using personal data that produces legal or similarly significant effects on data subjects
Data Types Involved: Personal data used as input to algorithmic or ML-based decisions (e.g., credit scoring, candidate screening, risk scoring)
Regulations Triggered: GDPR Art. 22; Art. 28; Art. 35 (DPIA likely required)
Required DPA / Contract Clauses: Art. 28 DPA; DPIA mandatory; DPA must include: nature of automated processing; logic involved; significance and envisaged consequences; data subject rights support (right to human review, explanation, contest)
Additional Obligations: DPIA required before deployment. Legal and Privacy Team must assess Art. 22 applicability. Vendor must support data subject rights obligations.

Trigger Condition: Vendor engagement involves only anonymized or fully aggregated data with no re-identification risk
Data Types Involved: Anonymized data (not personal data under GDPR if truly anonymous)
Regulations Triggered: GDPR Recital 26 (anonymization standard); risk of re-identification assessment required
Required DPA / Contract Clauses: No DPA required if data is genuinely anonymous. Anonymization methodology must be documented and validated. Lichen retains right to audit anonymization controls. Pseudonymized data is still personal data and is NOT exempt.
Additional Obligations: Privacy Team must validate anonymization methodology before classifying as out-of-scope. Document validation and retain.

Trigger Condition: Vendor processes only Lichen's own business data with no personal data component (e.g., machine telemetry, aggregate inventory counts, non-personal operational data)
Data Types Involved: Non-personal business data (no natural persons identifiable directly or indirectly)
Regulations Triggered: No personal data regulation triggered
Required DPA / Contract Clauses: No DPA required. Standard confidentiality provisions and NDA sufficient. Confirm data classification does not include personal data at any processing stage (including metadata, logs, and derived outputs).
Additional Obligations: Business Owner to confirm absence of personal data in data flows. IT Security to validate that logs and metadata do not introduce personal data.

Trigger Condition: Vendor experiences a personal data breach affecting Lichen personal data
Data Types Involved: Any personal data within scope of the DPA
Regulations Triggered: GDPR Art. 28(3)(f); Art. 33 (72-hour supervisory authority notification); Art. 34 (data subject notification where required); applicable US state breach notification laws
Required DPA / Contract Clauses: DPA must include: vendor obligation to notify Lichen without undue delay (Lichen standard: within 24 hours of vendor becoming aware); notification content requirements (nature of breach, categories, approximate number of data subjects and records, likely consequences, measures taken); obligation to cooperate with Lichen's breach response
Additional Obligations: Legal / Privacy Team leads breach response. IT Security coordinates forensic assessment. Regulatory notification timeline (72 hours for GDPR) begins from when Lichen becomes aware.
Lichen Action / Owner: Owner: Privacy Team / Legal / IT Security

Trigger Condition: Vendor receives a data subject request (access, deletion, portability, correction, restriction, objection) relating to personal data it processes on Lichen's behalf
Data Types Involved: Personal data within scope of the DPA relating to the requesting data subject
Regulations Triggered: GDPR Arts. 12–22; CCPA §1798.100–.125; UK GDPR equivalent provisions
Required DPA / Contract Clauses: DPA must include: vendor obligation to promptly (within 5 business days per Lichen standard) forward data subject requests to Lichen; vendor obligation to provide technical assistance to enable Lichen to fulfill requests; vendor must not respond directly to data subjects without Lichen authorization (unless legally compelled)
Additional Obligations: Lichen Privacy Team is responsible for fulfilling data subject requests. Vendor provides data-retrieval and deletion assistance within agreed SLA.

Trigger Condition: Vendor receives a legally binding government or law enforcement request for Lichen personal data
Data Types Involved: Personal data within scope of the DPA
Regulations Triggered: GDPR Art. 28(3)(a); applicable national law; potential conflict with Art. 48 (no judgment or decision of a non-EU court/authority shall be recognized unless international agreement)
Required DPA / Contract Clauses: DPA should include: obligation to notify Lichen of government requests unless legally prohibited; obligation to challenge overbroad requests where legally possible; Art. 48 conflict-of-law considerations if vendor is in a country with expansive surveillance laws (cf. FISA §702 / CLOUD Act for US-based vendors)
Additional Obligations: Legal / External Counsel to assess FISA / CLOUD Act exposure for US-based vendors handling EU data. Include notification and challenge obligations in DPA.

Trigger Condition: Vendor processes personal data under a DPA and Lichen wishes to exercise audit rights
Data Types Involved: Personal data within scope of the DPA
Regulations Triggered: GDPR Art. 28(3)(h) — controller audit right; Art. 5(2) accountability principle
Required DPA / Contract Clauses: DPA must include: right for Lichen (or appointed auditor) to conduct audits and inspections; vendor obligation to provide all information necessary to demonstrate compliance; right to conduct on-site audits with reasonable notice (Lichen standard: 30 days); vendor may satisfy audit right via third-party audit reports (e.g., SOC 2 Type II, ISO 27001) subject to Lichen acceptance
Additional Obligations: IT Security and Privacy Team coordinate audits. SOC 2 Type II report review is the standard first step. On-site audit reserved for RESTRICTED data vendors or post-incident.

Trigger Condition: Vendor will implement a DPIA as a joint exercise with Lichen for high-risk processing activities
Data Types Involved: High-risk personal data processing (Art. 35 criteria: large-scale, systematic monitoring, special categories, automated decision-making with significant effects)
Regulations Triggered: GDPR Art. 35; Art. 36 (prior consultation with supervisory authority if DPIA indicates high residual risk)
Required DPA / Contract Clauses: DPA must include vendor cooperation obligation for DPIA; vendor must provide technical and organizational details required for DPIA completion; if DPIA identifies high residual risk, Lichen must consult supervisory authority before processing (Art. 36)
Additional Obligations: Privacy Team leads DPIA. Vendor provides processing details. If Art. 36 consultation required, allow 8-week minimum timeline before processing.
